assertions.text: V1 was traveling northbound on the roadway (travel direction stated) [no specific speed value given]
V1 was negotiating a left-hand curve when traveling northbound
V1 departed the roadway on the right side (departure side relative to travel direction)
The front plane of V1 contacted a utility pole (impact with pole at vehicle front)
Impact with the utility pole occurred after V1 departed the roadway
No explicit claim of braking is made (braking not stated)
A single impact occurred: V1's front contacted one utility pole (one distinct impact described)
V1 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway partially in both the northbound and southbound lanes
At final rest V1 was facing southeast (post-impact orientation)

verdicts.evidence: The EDR speed channel shows V1 traveling at [83.0 km/h at t=-4.7 s](T2) and [80.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), consistent with northbound travel direction stated (direction itself is not encoded in telemetry but speed prior to impact is shown).
Roadway configuration (two-lane, two-way) is not recorded in vehicle telemetry; no sensor provides lane count or opposing-flow info, so this is [unverifiable].

Steering shows a peak raw value of [27.0 deg at t=-0.2 s](T2) and baseline median [4.5 deg](T2), indicating steering input, but telemetry alone cannot determine road curvature or left/right curve orientation relative to northbound travel without map alignment, so this is [unverifiable].

Lane departure side (right side) is a positional road-relative claim not recorded by vehicle sensors; GPS trajectory or lane offset is not available in the provided channels, so this is [unverifiable].

Crash-pulse shows a large longitudinal delta-V with final value [−75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2) indicating a high-speed frontal impact (EDR delta_v_long is along vehicle forward axis in device frame) and the EDR summary notes an airbag deployment stage at [3.5 ms](T1), consistent with a front impact with a pole.

Sequence 'departure then impact' cannot be confirmed solely from the vehicle sensors provided; telemetry shows the impact at [t=0](T1) but lacks an independent signal for roadway departure timing or position, so this is [unverifiable].
Brake channel statistics show fraction_on [0.0 over t=-4.7 to 0.25 s](T2) with no transitions, supporting that braking was not recorded prior to impact.

Detect events and delta-V channels show a single dominant crash pulse with longitudinal max delta-V [−75.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2) and no additional separate impact pulses reported (detect_events found one major delta-v event)(T3), consistent with one primary impact.

Post-impact rotation direction (clockwise) requires orientation/heading data after impact which is not present; gyro-integrated rotation is not provided as vehicle-frame heading so rotation sense relative to map is [unverifiable].
Final rest position (middle of roadway across north/south lanes) is a spatial claim not recorded in the provided telemetry channels, so this is [unverifiable].
Final vehicle heading (facing southeast) is not available in the provided channels; sensor gyros give integrated rotation but without map alignment the absolute heading is [unverifiable].

result.rationale: Telemetry strongly supports a high-speed frontal impact with no recorded braking and a single major crash pulse ([−75.6 km/h delta-V](T2); speed prior to impact [83.0→80.0 km/h](T2); brake off [fraction_on 0.0](T2)). Positional and directional claims about lane configuration, departure side, curve handedness, and final resting orientation are not verifiable from the available vehicle-frame sensors.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
